LAW AND MOTION 302, TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THIS WILL EXTEND THE TRO & REQUIRE CALSTERS TO CONTINUE PAYING PETITIONERS' PENSIONS UNTIL THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCESS IS COMPLETE. CCSF, CITY COLLEGE IS NOT AN INDISPENSABLE PARTY. THERE IS MERIT IN THE DUE PROCESS CLAIM. THERE IS ALSO ENOUGH OF A RECORD TO CONCLUDE THAT EACH OF THE PETITIONER NEEDS THE MONEY TO SURVIVE. THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THIS PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION WILL PREJUDICE CALSTERS. NO OTHER RELIEF IS GRANTED AS PETITIONERS HAVE NOT EXHAUSTED THEIR ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES YET. IF A PARTY WANTS A STATEMENT OF DECISION, THAT PARTY SHOULD PREPARE ONE & SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER WHICH COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312, APPROVAL AS TO FORM.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Off Calendar

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susan M. Carson, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5580, for defendant;
David P. Clisham, Esq. of Clisham & Sortor, Tel. 415.291.0855, for petitioners;
Justine L. Clisham, Esq. of Clisham & Sortor, Tel. 415.291.0855, for petitioners;
Phil Crawford, Esq. of the Law Office of Phil Crawford, Tel. 408.313.3212, for petitioners.

RULING: TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: The preliminary injunction is granted. This will extend the TRO & require CalSTERS to continue paying petitioners' pensions until the administrative process is complete. CCSF, City College is not an indispensable party. There is merit in the due process claim. There is also enough of a record to conclude that each of the petitioner needs the money to survive. There is no showing that this preliminary injunction will prejudice CalSTERS. No other relief is granted as petitioners have not exhausted their administrative remedies yet. If a party wants a statement of decision, that party should prepare one & submit it to the court. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order which complies with CRC 3.1312, approval as to form.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Alternative Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susan M. Carson, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5580, for defendant;
David P. Clisham, Esq. of Clisham & Sortor, Tel. 415.291.0855, for petitioners;
Justine L. Clisham, Esq. of Clisham & Sortor, Tel. 415.291.0855, for petitioners;
Phil Crawford, Esq. of the Law Office of Phil Crawford, Tel. 408.313.3212, for petitioners.

RULING: TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE - A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: In light of the ruling granting preliminary injunction, the request re: alternative writ of mandate is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order which complies with CRC 3.1312, approval as to form.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
